title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
summary: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that code alone is insufficient competitive moat; customer context, proprietary data, and operational complexity at scale provide the real advantage. He contends that fears of AI disrupting SaaS companies are overstated and that the best AI products succeed by creating raving fans rather than simply satisfying customers.
writeup: Gong CEO Amit Bendov makes the case that code is just one piece of a software moat, with durable advantage coming from customer context, proprietary data, product experience, and operational scale. The discussion pushes back on the claim that AI will wipe out SaaS companies, framing that worry as overstated. The takeaway: in an era of fast, AI-assisted coding, the products that win cultivate passionate customer bases rather than settling for mere satisfaction.
